summary.intent: Implement NIP-59 Gift Wrap support for Nostr DMs in the moltis-nostr crate, replacing deprecated NIP-04 (kind:4) with privacy-preserving gift-wrapped messages (kind:1059) while maintaining backward compatibility for inbound messages.
summary.outcome: Full NIP-59 implementation completed: new gift_wrap.rs module, updated inbound subscription and routing in bus.rs, outbound send_text migrated to gift wraps, OTP functions updated, integration tests added. All 367 workspace tests pass, clippy and rustfmt clean.

repo: nostr-sdk 0.44 has NIP-59 support behind a `nip59` feature flag; the feature must be explicitly added alongside existing `nip04` and `nip44` features in the workspace Cargo.toml
Gift wrap timestamps are randomly tweaked 0–2 days, requiring a wider `since` window (172800 seconds) on relay subscription filters to avoid missing recent messages
The codebase uses a custom `Error::Encryption` variant that can be reused for NIP-59 errors — no new error variants needed
Clippy is configured with `deny(expect_used)` in test modules, requiring `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` annotations when using `.expect()` in tests
A pinned nightly toolchain is used for `rustfmt` (invoked via `just format`)

code.finding: Subscription filter originally subscribed only to kind:4; restructured to use `.kinds()` accepting both Kind::EncryptedDirectMessage and Kind::GiftWrap in a single filter

handle_event restructured to extract (sender, plaintext, timestamp) early via kind-specific routing, then run a unified pipeline for dedup, self-msg check, stale check, OTP, access control, and dispatch
handle_otp_verification and handle_otp_challenge previously used inline nip04::encrypt; both now delegate to gift_wrap::send_gift_wrapped_dm()
send_text replaced NIP-04 encrypt + kind:4 event publish with a single call to crate::gift_wrap::send_gift_wrapped_dm()
New shared module: send_gift_wrapped_dm() uses EventBuilder::private_msg(); unwrap_gift_wrap() uses nip59::extract_rumor() and validates rumor kind is PrivateDirectMessage (14)

workflow: nostr-sdk's client.send_event() takes a reference (&event), not an owned value — a compile error caught this
The nostr-sdk subscribe API takes a single Filter, not a Vec<Filter>; multiple kinds must be combined via .kinds() on one filter
Incremental compile-check-fix cycle (cargo check → fix API mismatches → cargo test → fix lint → rustfmt) was effective for catching SDK API differences from plan assumptions
The `just lint` and `just format-check` commands wrap clippy and rustfmt respectively for the workspace

friction: Two nostr-sdk API mismatches discovered at compile time: send_event() requires a reference, and subscribe() takes a single Filter not a Vec — the plan assumed a multi-filter subscribe API that doesn't exist
Clippy `expect_used` denial in tests required adding `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` to the gift_wrap unit test module — not anticipated in the plan
Integration test had a collapsible_if lint and formatting issues that required an extra fix-and-recheck cycle
open_items: No config toggle exists for outbound protocol — NIP-59 is now the only outbound path; a toggle for legacy NIP-04 outbound could be added later if needed for compatibility
send_and_receive_gift_wrapped_dm integration test is marked #[ignore] and requires a live relay — not covered in CI
Staleness check for gift wrap inbound uses rumor.created_at (not outer timestamp) — this logic should be validated against edge cases with real relay behavior
